reason: Bitcoin demonstrates resilience and emerging safe-haven characteristics amidst global macro uncertainty (oil price volatility, traditional market instability), evidenced by increased volume on Japanese exchanges. While institutional interest (Farage stake) adds long-term legitimacy, immediate upside is tempered by ongoing regulatory ambiguity (Clarity Act potentially favoring banks) and broader economic risks. The market appears to be consolidating, absorbing these mixed signals, suggesting an accumulation phase for strategic positioning.
headlines: Nigel Farage takes 6% stake in UK bitcoin treasury firm Stack BTC
Clarity Act will benefit banks more than crypto, former CFTC chair says
Bitflyer volume surges 200% past Binance, Coinbase as oil spike sends Nikkei sliding
Oil pulls back from 25% spike as G7 discusses emergency reserve release
Bitcoin steadies as limited U.S. exposure to oil shocks calms markets
